location.office_time_required: three days a week (Tuesdays through Thursdays) from HQ in Union Square, Manhattan

level_signals: 5+ years of operations, program, or project management experience
Acts as strategic thought partner to Technical Solutions leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of operations, program, or project management experience in a SaaS or technical environment
must_haves.qualifier: in a SaaS or technical environment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience helping teams leverage new tooling, particularly AI capabilities
Proven ability to drive alignment across multiple teams
Strong operational instincts — you can see what's broken and build something to fix it
Excellent communication and facilitation skills; comfortable presenting to senior leadership
Technical fluency — you don't need to write code, but you're excited to dig into how our tech works
Thrives in ambiguity and knows how to create structure where there isn't any yet

scope.surface: Technical Solutions vertical (Solutions Engineering, Implementation Management, Technical Account Management, Technical Support, Solutions Architecture)
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS — identity risk / fraud, credit, and compliance platform for financial institutions and fintechs
primary_metrics_inferred: Operational efficiency across Technical Solutions teams
Data-informed decision-making on product, process, and resourcing
Alignment and execution quality on cross-functional strategic initiatives
core_responsibilities: Act as a strategic thought partner to Technical Solutions leadership
Build and manage operational cadences and structures
Empower teams to leverage tooling, particularly AI capabilities, to improve the way we work
Own a portfolio of cross-functional programs, defining strategy, milestones, and success metrics for each
Serve as the connective tissue between GTM, Technical Solutions, Product, and Engineering
Manage internal and external stakeholder relationships, including partner-facing touchpoints during project execution
Communicate program status, risks, and decisions clearly to leadership and working teams
Establish Operational Rhythms: Design and implement operational structures and cadences
Enable Data-Informed Decision Making: Establish systems, processes and tools to understand and improve the way we work
Own execution of strategic projects that optimize internal and client/partner interactions

green_flags: Equity (ISOs) explicitly offered in addition to base salary
Clear scope of ownership — Technical Solutions vertical end-to-end
Specific cross-functional partners named (GTM, Product, Engineering)
Lean team described as high-impact opportunity
Strong benefits package including 16 weeks paid parental leave, 401k match, and L&D stipend
red_flags: Hybrid requires Tuesdays through Thursdays onsite in Manhattan — eliminates remote and non-NYC candidates
Visa sponsorship not mentioned; unclear if available
No nice-to-haves listed, making it harder to gauge relative priority of requirements
Role requires building from scratch with ambiguity — may be stressful without strong existing infrastructure

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + years + scope)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
compensation.location_pay_differential (inferred from JD language 'pay may vary depending on … geography')
ambiguities: The JD mentions 'hybrid-work environment' with three days onsite, but also states 'On Monday/Friday most employees Zoom into work from home while some take advantage of the quieter office' — implies some flexibility on non-core days but three core days are firm.
No nice-to-haves section exists in the JD; all listed requirements were treated as must-haves given unconditional language used throughout the 'Who we're looking for' section.
Archetype is borderline between Technical Program Manager and BizOps/Strategy & Ops; labeled TPM given explicit program ownership and cross-functional execution focus, with BizOps as secondary.
